# World Map: [GAME_TITLE]

<!-- Spatial hierarchy: World → Region → Area → Location → Scene -->

---

## Spatial Model

**Model type**: [Linear / Hub-and-Spoke / Open-World]
<!-- Linear      — Regions unlock in a fixed sequence; no backtracking
     Hub-and-Spoke — Central hub Region with radiating optional Regions
     Open-World  — All Regions accessible from start; progression is content-gated -->

**Total Regions**: [N]
**Total Areas**: [N]
**Total Locations**: [N]
**Total Scenes (NODE-xxx)**: [N]

---

## Region Registry

<!-- One entry per Region. Regions are the top-level spatial unit.
     They unlock per act / quest stage / player decision.
     REGION-ID format: REGION-{ShortName} e.g. REGION-Thornwood -->

| Region ID | Region Name | Theme | Unlock Condition | Acts Present | Area Count | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| REGION-[ShortName] | [Name] | [Atmosphere/theme in 3 words] | [start / after quest X / faction rep >= N] | Act [N]–[N] | [N] | |
| REGION-[ShortName] | [Name] | | | | | |

## Region Details

### REGION-[ShortName]: [Region Name]

**Theme**: [Narrative and atmospheric theme of this region]
**Unlock condition**: [When/how the player gains access]
**Acts present**: Act [N] through Act [N]
**Narrative role**: [What story function does this region serve?]
**Dominant faction**: [Faction controlling this region, if any]
**Faction reputation gate**: [Minimum rep to enter / operate freely]

**Fast travel available?**: [Yes / No / After discovery]
**Rest locations**: [List LOC-xxx that allow short/long rest]

## Area → Location → Scene Index

<!-- Quick-reference index of the full spatial hierarchy.
     Detailed entries live in specs/[FEATURE_DIR]/areas/ and specs/[FEATURE_DIR]/locations.md -->

```
REGION-[Name]
├── AREA-[Name]  ([type])
│   ├── LOC-[Name]  ([location type])
│   │   ├── NODE-1001_[SceneName]  (dialogue)
│   │   ├── NODE-1002_[SceneName]  (combat)
│   │   └── NODE-1003_[SceneName]  (quest_event)
│   └── LOC-[Name]  ([location type])
│       └── NODE-1010_[SceneName]  (exploration)
└── AREA-[Name]  ([type])
    └── LOC-[Name]  ([location type])
        ├── NODE-1020_[SceneName]  (travel)  → REGION-[Next]
        └── NODE-1021_[SceneName]  (rest)
```

---

## Travel Connections

<!-- Inter-Region and inter-Area travel routes.
     One row per directional connection.
     "one-way" = player cannot return via this route.
     Travel scenes (NODE-xxx, scene_type: travel) implement these connections in prose. -->

| From | To | Via | Direction | Travel Node | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| REGION-[A] | REGION-[B] | [Road / Portal / Sea / Dungeon exit] | two-way / one-way | NODE-NNN | |
| LOC-[A] | LOC-[B] | [path name] | two-way | NODE-NNN | |

---

## Travel Encounter Tables

<!-- Populated when constitution.md travel_encounters: encounter_table.
     Defines what can happen when the player moves between Regions or Areas.
     speckit.compile reads this section to generate $travel_encounter_registry in StoryInit.
     Each region has its own encounter pool. Entries are drawn by weighted random roll
     (or left empty if the region is always safe).

     Roll mechanic: d20 + $region_[ShortName]_danger vs. travel_encounter_dc (constitution.md)
     Danger modifier increases story-dynamically (e.g. after alerting a faction).

     Encounter types:
       combat     — triggers a combat encounter (uses sugarcube-combat-template.twee)
       event      — navigates to a scripted passage (authored separately as a NODE)
       discovery  — grants an item or reveals a Location on the world map
       hazard     — applies damage or a condition without a full combat scene

     Leave the table empty ("entries: []") for regions that are always safe.
     Omit a region entirely if travel_encounters does not apply to it. -->

### REGION-[ShortName] Encounters

**Base danger modifier**: [N]  *(added to d20 roll; 0 = normal, 3 = threatening, 5 = deadly)*

| ID | Type | Weight | Display Name | Intro Prose | Details |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [encounter_id_1] | combat | [W] | [Name] | [One sentence flavour] | Enemies: [Name] CR[N] ×[count]; Loot: [container_id or none] |
| [encounter_id_2] | event | [W] | [Name] | [One sentence flavour] | Passage: [NODE-NNN_EventName] |
| [encounter_id_3] | discovery | [W] | [Name] | [One sentence flavour] | Item: [item_var] "[Display]" or LOC-[ID] revealed |
| [encounter_id_4] | hazard | [W] | [Name] | [One sentence flavour] | Damage: -[N] HP; Condition: [condition or none] |

### REGION-[ShortName] Encounters

**Base danger modifier**: [N]

| ID | Type | Weight | Display Name | Intro Prose | Details |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [encounter_id] | [type] | [W] | [Name] | [Flavour] | [Details] |

<!-- Add one ### section per Region that has travel encounters.
     Regions with travel_encounters: none can be omitted.
     in constitution.md that have no entry here. -->

## World State Variables

<!-- Variables that track spatial unlock and discovery state.
     Follow the naming convention: $world_*, $region_*, $area_*, $loc_* -->

| Variable | Scope | Type | Default | Set When |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| $world_[name] | Campaign | flag / counter | false / 0 | [Condition] |
| $region_[name]_unlocked | Region | flag | false | [Unlock condition] |
| $area_[name]_explored | Area | counter (0–100) | 0 | [On each LOC visit] |
| $loc_[name]_visited | Location | flag | false | [First visit] |
| $loc_[name]_state | Location | string | "intact" | [Event changes it] |
